full_response:
Let  

v(λ)=⟨x(λ),y(λ),z(λ)⟩=⟨sin (λ²), ln (λ+1), 2λ²−1⟩ ,    λ>−1.  

--------------------------------------------------------------------
1. Differentiate each component

x′(λ)=d/dλ [sin (λ²)]=cos (λ²)·2λ=2λ cos (λ²)

y′(λ)=d/dλ [ln (λ+1)]=1/(λ+1)

z′(λ)=d/dλ [2λ²−1]=4λ

Hence  

v′(λ)=⟨2λ cos (λ²), 1/(λ+1), 4λ⟩.
--------------------------------------------------------------------
2. Compute the cross–product v×v′

Write the determinant

v×v′ =
|  i     j    k |
| sin (λ²) ln(λ+1) 2λ²−1 |
| 2λcos (λ²) 1/(λ+1)  4λ |

(i)  i–component  
   = ln(λ+1)·4λ − (2λ²−1)·(1/(λ+1))  
   = 4λ ln(λ+1) − (2λ²−1)/(λ+1).

(ii) j–component (remember the minus sign)  
   = −[ sin (λ²)·4λ − (2λ²−1)·2λ cos (λ²) ]  
   = −4λ sin (λ²) + 2λ cos (λ²)(2λ²−1).

(iii) k–component  
   = sin (λ²)·1/(λ+1) − ln(λ+1)·2λ cos (λ²).

--------------------------------------------------------------------
3. Result

v(λ)×v′(λ)=⟨ 4λ ln(λ+1) − (2λ²−1)/(λ+1), 
             −4λ sin (λ²) + 2λ cos (λ²)(2λ²−1), 
             sin (λ²)/(λ+1) − 2λ cos (λ²) ln(λ+1) ⟩.

\boxed{\,v(\lambda)\times v'(\lambda)=\bigl(4\lambda\ln(\lambda+1)-\tfrac{2\lambda^{2}-1}{\lambda+1},\;
-4\lambda\sin(\lambda^{2})+2\lambda\cos(\lambda^{2})(2\lambda^{2}-1),\;
\tfrac{\sin(\lambda^{2})}{\lambda+1}-2\lambda\cos(\lambda^{2})\ln(\lambda+1)\bigr)\,}

Confidence: 0.87
